summary: BenchmarkDotNet-driven performance guidance for operators, subjects, schedulers, and allocation-sensitive reactive pipelines.
recommendedPatterns: Measure operator and subject choices in realistic workloads before optimizing.
Use benchmark suites to catch regressions in throughput and allocations.
Treat replay, buffering, and multicasting choices as performance-affecting decisions.
avoidPatterns: Do not assume two operators or subject types have identical cost profiles.
Do not optimize reactive pipelines without representative benchmarks.
setupSteps: Create focused BenchmarkDotNet suites around the operators or subjects under scrutiny.
Benchmark at multiple observer counts and event counts.
commonPitfalls: Choosing ReplaySubject<T> without understanding replay retention cost.
Ignoring the effect of observer count on subject overhead.

sources.title: Performance Benchmarks (Embedded Offline Catalog)
sources.notes: Embedded offline summary curated into the Reactive MCP Server knowledge catalog.

harvest.sourceSummary: Curated into an embedded offline catalog for Reactive MCP Server from prior research and normalized for local-only runtime use.
